description_long: This research institute building features a modern, geometric design with a strong emphasis on horizontal lines. The facade is composed of alternating bands of olive green and beige, with prominent white horizontal strips separating each floor. The structure is boxy and symmetrical, with a recessed central section at the top that suggests a possible rooftop terrace or atrium. The windows are arranged in a grid pattern, giving the building a uniform and institutional appearance. The overall look is utilitarian, with minimal ornamentation and a focus on functionality.
description: A modern, boxy research institute with olive green and beige horizontal bands, white separators, and a grid of windows.
description_view_0: Front view showing the symmetrical facade with horizontal bands and a recessed rooftop section.
description_view_1: Side view nearly identical to the front, highlighting the uniform grid of windows and banding.
description_view_2: Rear view, similar to the front, with the same banded design and recessed rooftop.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, mirroring the other sides with consistent horizontal bands and window grids.
attribute_entryway_design: The main entryway is not clearly visible in any view; no doors, steps, or decorative elements are apparent from the exterior.
